Ground Control for
Uncrewed Systems

Unified Ground Control Station
==============================
Unified Ground Control Station (UGCS)

Transportable
Ground Station
=================================
Transportable Ground Station (TGS)

ISR Mission Planner
===================
ISR Mission Planner

items.notes: Core products are GCS hardware (TGS, KTAC, KED) and software (UGCS, ISR Mission Planner, Unified Video Terminal, STANAG 4586 Interop). Homepage hero: 'Ground Control for Uncrewed Systems'.

evidence.snippet: Our auto-router, sensor footprint, radio frequency line-of-sight, and route-and-area survey algorithm can be combined to create a powerful autonomy engine for your uncrewed systems.

*   Designed for integration on Uncrewed Aerial Systems (UAS) and ground-based Vehicle Specific Modules (VSM) to achieve STANAG 4586 compliance
*   Unifies the interfaces between the Command and Control (C2) elements on the ground and in the air to provide a common control interface across all classes and types of uncrewed aircraft

items.notes: Advanced Autonomy software provides auto-routing, airspace management, flight planning engine. SMC is designed for onboard UAS integration to achieve STANAG 4586 compliance across air and ground elements.

products_categories.notes: Vendor focus is UAS command & control — hardware GCS and software. SMC also functions as an airborne STANAG 4586 integration computer.

value.address: 2075 W Pinnacle Peak Rd, Suite 102, Phoenix, AZ 85027

evidence.snippet:
**ARIZONA Corporate Headquarters**
2075 W Pinnacle Peak Rd,
Suite 102
Phoenix, AZ 85027

Mission Critical Technologies
-----------------------------
Kutta actively pursues research and development projects, creates successful partnerships with commercial customers, and develops new technologies that enhance safety for Uncrewed Aerial Systems (UAS). Our experience and expertise in the latest technology trends and designs allow our customers to further their own product lines and integrate our products into their systems.

Unified Ground Control Station
==============================
Unified Ground Control Station (UGCS)

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Sells major UAS subsystems — GCS hardware (TGS HMMWV shelter, KTAC handheld controller, SMC mission computer, KED) and GCS software (UGCS, ISR MP, Advanced Autonomy). Does not manufacture complete UAS platforms. Subsidiary of SNC.

evidence.snippet:
### Kutta Tactical Controller (KTAC)
The KTAC provides soldiers universal control for all types of uncrewed systems. The rugged, modular design uses smartphone technology and is designed to meet the environmental challenges of expeditionary on-the-move operations.

Small Mission Computer (SMC)
----------------------------
The SMC is a ruggedized, general-purpose computing device that can be used in a wide variety of applications.

Transportable
Ground Station
=================================
Transportable Ground Station (TGS)

items.name: MUM-178

evidence.snippet: Kutta Technologies, Inc. has announced its new Manned / Unmanned Teaming Kit (MUM-178). The MUM-178 toolkit is one of the first products of its kind available to help facilitate the teaming of both manned and unmanned assets and it was developed based on DO-178C principles.

items.notes: 2014 news item; may be legacy or superseded by current UGCS MUM-T features.

products.notes: Product detail pages for KTAC, KED, Unified Video Terminal, and STANAG 4586 Interoperability were skipped in the crawl. Their inclusion here is based on site nav and product sheet listings.

ndaa.notes: No NDAA Section 848/889 claim found in fetched pages. Vendor is a subsidiary of SNC (Sierra Nevada Corporation), a US defense firm, but no explicit NDAA compliance claim is made on the site.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS / Blue sUAS mention found in any fetched page.

tagline: Phoenix-based ground-segment and flight-control subsystem supplier producing STANAG 4586–compliant command-and-control software (UGCS variants), rugged handheld controllers (KTAC), mission computers, autonomy engines, and ISR planning tools. Production-ready across hardware and software. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of integration partnerships and government use cases is limited.
